senses.definition: In a way that relates to the period before the onset of a disease or morbid condition.
examples: The patient was premorbidly healthy, with no prior history of illness.
Premorbidly, she led an active lifestyle before the accident.

senses.definition: The state of being in relation to health or illness before the onset of a disease; pre-morbid condition.
examples: His premorbidness was good, with no prior medical complaints.
Assessing premorbidness helps doctors understand baseline health.

senses.definition: The period before morning; predawn or early morning hours.
examples: He woke at premorning to watch the sunrise.
The village is quiet during premorning.

senses.definition: In a state before being broken or wounded; not yet morse/embossed; note: this is an extremely rare or specialized term.
examples: The term premorse is seldom used outside of specialized contexts.

senses.definition: Relating to the time before death; existing or occurring before mortality.
examples: Premortal considerations influenced the hospice plan.
The premortal phase of the organism is poorly understood.

senses.definition: In a premortal manner; before death or mortality.
examples: The patient was premortally ill but coherent until the end.
Premortally, he discussed his wishes with the family.

senses.definition: The act or process of preparing for death or the state before death; a preceding form of mortification.
examples: The documentary explores premortification as a cultural practice.
Scholars discuss premortification in ancient texts.

senses.definition: Mortified in anticipation of death; having undergone premortification.
examples: Premortified feelings overwhelmed him as the prognosis was announced.
She described premortified emotions before retirement.

senses.definition: To cause or undergo mortification before death; to preemptively mortify.
examples: The ritual premortify their belongings.
Protesters premortify the scandal by revealing documents.

senses.definition: Causing mortification in advance; acting to preemptively mortify.
examples: Premortifying rituals were performed before the battle.
They engaged in premortifying acts to prevent later shame.

senses.definition: Relating to or situated before death; in or near a place associated with death or mortuary functions.
examples: The premortuary wing housed artifacts from earlier eras.
They studied premortuary practices across cultures.

senses.definition: An early, pre-larval stage in the development of certain organisms, prior to morula formation.
examples: Biologists observed premorula cells dividing before formation of the morula.
Premorula stages are studied in embryology.

senses.definition: An image or pattern formed before a mosaic; a preliminary mosaic or mosaic-like stage.
examples: The artist showed a premosaic sketch before the final tiling.
Premosaic planning helped align the final design.

senses.definition: A prior motion or proposal; an initial suggestion or impulse before formal movement.
examples: The committee discussed premotion ideas before voting.
Premotion influenced the final policy direction.

senses.definition: Multiple premotion ideas or proposals; initial impulses before formal motions.
examples: Several premotions were discussed in the early meeting.
The council weighed all premotions before proceeding.

senses.definition: One who comes before or moves something earlier; a precursor mover.
examples: The premover of the policy is credited with initiating reform.
As a premover, she set the agenda for the session.

senses.definition: Actions taken before a move; pre-move actions in games or processes.
examples: In chess, premoves can save time, but may backfire.
Premoves in the software help speed up workflows.

senses.definition: Acting to move something earlier; preemptively moving.
examples: They were premoving the schedule to avoid delays.
Premoving resources can reduce bottlenecks.

senses.definition: Abbreviation or shorthand; context-dependent, could refer to premorbid or premisses in specific domains.
examples: The file contains several prems that need clarification.
